narrative.headline: 2018 Chevrolet Colorado: power train leads owner complaints
narrative.summary: Across 175 owner complaints to NHTSA, the power train is the most-reported area by a wide margin at 75 reports (43%), well ahead of the electrical system (32, 18%) and engine (22, 13%). The power train reports include 1 crash and 2 injuries, the highest count of any category here. Three other areas each note a single fire: the electrical system, the engine, and the fuel/propulsion system.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Most-reported area by far: 75 complaints (43%), with 1 crash and 2 injuries, the most of any category. One owner describes the parked truck rolling down a slight incline.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 32 complaints (18%), including 1 fire. One owner reports the backup-camera display stuck at full brightness, with the dimming knob having no effect at night.

issues.sample: WHILE DRIVING HOME, YELLOW CHECK ENGINE LIGHT CAME ON & I NOTICED REDUCTION OF OIL PRESSURE.  PARKED IN DRIVEWAY, SHUT OFF ENGINE, WENT INSIDE.  UPON RETURNING, NOTICED SIGNIFICANT AMOUNT OF OIL ON DRIVEWAY.  DIPSTICK SHOWED NO OIL.  TOWED TO DEALERSHIP.  THEY VERIFIED LEAKING CA
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 22 complaints (13%), including 1 fire. One owner reports a check-engine light, dropping oil pressure, and a major oil leak that emptied the engine.

issues.sample: UPON ACCELERATING THE VEHICLE ONTO A 3 LANE ROAD, ALL WARNING LIGHTS CAME ON IN THE VEHICLE.  A WARNING MESSAGE PRESENTED ON THE DASHBOARD STATING "ENGINE POWER REDUCTION" AND THEN THE VEHICLE SHUT DOWN COMPLETELY WHILE MOVING.  THE ENGINE SHUT OFF AND THE VEHICLE WOULD NOT DISEN
issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 18 complaints (10%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. One owner describes all warning lights coming on and the vehicle shutting down while moving.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 10 complaints (6%), including 1 fire. One owner reports a transmission shudder during light acceleration between 30 and 70 mph.
